findings.message: New write endpoint POST /items/{item_id}/archive has no domain-level instrumentation: no structured log with correlation id is emitted for the archive action, no counter is incremented for archives performed, and no span/event is recorded. The app-level ObservabilityMiddleware only captures generic HTTP timing/status — a state-mutating business operation is otherwise invisible in metrics/logs/traces.
findings.suggestion: Acquire the project logger (get_logger()) and emit log.info("item_archived", item_id=item_id) on success; consider adding a counter (e.g. app_items_archived_total) to the in-process MetricsRegistry or recoverability module and incrementing it on success so the action is visible on /metrics and in Grafana.

findings.message: The 404 not-found error path raises HTTPException without logging the failure or correlating it to a request id. Error paths must be logged with the correlation id per the observability contract; relying on the middleware to count it as a non-5xx status hides the business-meaningful 'archive of missing item' failure mode.
findings.suggestion: Before raising HTTPException, emit a structured warning log including item_id and the request's correlation id (e.g. log.warning("archive_item_not_found", item_id=item_id)). Consider also bumping a domain error counter so 404s on this endpoint are observable distinctly from generic 4xx.

findings.message: session.commit() can raise (IntegrityError, OperationalError, etc.) and the exception will surface as an unhandled 500 with no context-rich log at the call site. There is no try/except that logs the failure with the correlation id and item_id before re-raising, so debugging archive failures relies solely on the global handler's generic counter.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the commit in try/except, log.exception("archive_item_commit_failed", item_id=item_id) (which preserves the correlation id via the structured logger context), then re-raise so the global handler still records the unhandled-exception metric.

findings.message: No explicit tracing span is opened around the archive operation. With APP_OTEL_ENABLED, FastAPI auto-instrumentation produces a request span but no child span describing the archive sub-operation or attributes (item_id, archived=True/False). State-mutating endpoints benefit from explicit span attributes for trace-based debugging.
findings.suggestion: If OTel is enabled, obtain a tracer (trace.get_tracer(__name__)) and wrap the archive logic in a span (e.g. with tracer.start_as_current_span("archive_item") as span: span.set_attribute("item.id", item_id)) so the operation is queryable in Tempo by attribute.
